log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, 760KB, compiled with Borland Delphi. It has an overlay section with high entropy (6.51), suggesting potential packing or encryption. I need to get all IOC types to und

The IOC summary shows no network activity, 1 registry persistence key, 2 dropped files, and 0 processes created (though the process tree showed one). I need to investigate the dropped files, especiall

The malware dropped a file named 'svchost.com' in the Windows directory, which is a classic technique to masquerade as a legitimate system process. I need to investigate the registry changes to unders

The malware has established persistence by modifying the HKCR\exefile\shell\open\command registry key to execute 'C:\Windows\svchost.com' whenever any .exe file is double-clicked. This is a classic 'S

The process activity shows no child processes were created, but notable modules include Adobe Reader executables and shell32.dll, suggesting the malware may interact with these components. The mutex n

Network activity is empty, confirming this is a local-only malware with no C2 communication observed in the sandbox. The primary threat is the registry persistence mechanism that hijacks the 'Shell:Op
